page: Microsoft Azure DC96ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the DCadsv6 family. It is powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration includes 3686 GB of local storage capacity and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated graphics processing units (GPUs) on this instance, making it reliant entirely on its CPU resources for computational tasks.

From a resource density perspective, the DC96ads_v6 offers a high-core, balanced-memory profile suitable for enterprise-scale workloads. The inclusion of substantial local storage alongside dedicated CPU allocation provides a stable environment for applications requiring consistent compute and local disk access. This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as large-scale general-purpose applications, database hosting, and enterprise backend services.
description: Microsoft Azure DC96ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the DCadsv6 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. For storage, the instance is equipped with 3686 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage configurations. It does not include any graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high core count and substantial local storage capacity make this instance suitable for resource-dense deployments, enterprise-scale general-purpose applications, and database hosting workloads requiring consistent CPU allocation and local disk performance.
